full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic wing rib for a conceptual-design two-seat STOL light aircraft (MTOM 2000 kg) with CS-23-adjacent load factors. The rib is located at mid-span, airfoil NACA 23012 with chord 1500 mm; overall rib height 180 mm (12% chord). The rib has 3 spar cutouts (two main spars + one auxiliary), 4 lightening holes, a fuel-tank inspection port 100 mm diameter, and 8 skin-attachment tabs around the perimeter on a nominal 180 mm pitch. Mounting interface: rib bolts to front spar via 4x AN4-3A bolts (6.35 mm dia) and to rear spar via 4x AN3-4A bolts (4.76 mm dia). Material: 2024-T3 aluminum sheet 1.6 mm thick, E = 73.1 GPa, yield = 345 MPa, ultimate = 483 MPa, density = 2780 kg/m^3. Load cases (CS-23-adjacent): positive limit load factor n1 = 3.8 g, ultimate = 1.5 x limit = 5.7 g. (R1) Positive ultimate 5.7 g with MTOM 2000 kg: spanwise-distributed lift at the rib station is 1.20 kN/m (elliptical, scaled to limit then ultimate); this produces a shear of 1.8 kN and a bending moment of 2.7 kN*m at the rib. Applied as distributed chordwise pressure on rib webs and transferred through spar cutouts. Max von Mises in rib <= yield = 345 MPa (ultimate factor of safety of 1.5 already built into the ultimate load). (R2) Negative limit 1.5 g inverted with 2000 kg MTOM: rib carries reverse bending; max von Mises <= yield = 345 MPa. (R3) Fuel inspection port boss stress concentration under R1: max stress concentration factor of 3.0 on a 100 mm hole in a 1.6 mm sheet; local max von Mises around port boss <= yield = 345 MPa. (R4) Rib mass <= 250 g. (R5) Linear buckling eigenvalue under ultimate compressive load flow through the rib web >= 1.5. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static for R1, R2, R3) + linear buckling (R5). Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation explicitly NOT required at this gate. Deliverables: STEP AP242, materials certificate, and a report listing rib mass in g, max von Mises per load case in MPa, first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic mid-span wing rib for a conceptual two-seat STOL light aircraft (MTOM 2000 kg). The rib is a 1.6 mm 2024-T3 sheet, NACA 23012 airfoil, chord 1500 mm, rib height 180 mm, with three spar cutouts, four lightening holes, a 100 mm fuel-tank inspection port, and eight skin-attachment tabs around the perimeter. It bolts to the front spar via 4x AN4-3A and to the rear spar via 4x AN3-4A.

Sized to CS-23-adjacent load factors: positive limit n1 = 3.8 g, ultimate = 1.5 x limit = 5.7 g.

Load cases.
- LC1 positive ultimate 5.7 g at MTOM 2000 kg: rib-station spanwise lift ~1.20 kN/m, giving ~1.8 kN shear and ~2.7 kN*m bending at the rib.
- LC2 negative limit 1.5 g inverted (reverse bending at MTOM).
- LC3 fuel-port boss stress concentration under LC1 (Kt ~ 3.0 on the 100 mm hole in a 1.6 mm sheet).
- LC4 linear buckling under ultimate compressive rib-web flow.

prompt.title: Light aircraft wing rib (mid-span, STOL 2000 kg MTOM)
prompt.brief: 2024-T3 1.6 mm sheet rib, NACA 23012 chord 1500 mm. Sized for 3.8 g limit / 5.7 g ultimate on 2000 kg MTOM airframe.

boundary_conditions.mounting: front and rear spar bolt holes constrained
boundary_conditions.loads: distributed pressure on rib web / concentrated at spar cutouts

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Aeroelastic and flutter are separate certification gates.
